title: Mexican Man Dies in ICE Custody in Laredo, Texas — 20th Detention Death of 2026
excerpt: Felix Alcorta-Rodriguez, 63, died in ICE custody at the Webb County Detention Center in Laredo, Texas — becoming at least the 20th person to die in ICE detention in 2026 and the fifth to die in Texas ICE custody this year, at a facility that had been cited for at least nine violations in an ICE inspection just months earlier.
content:
Felix Alcorta-Rodriguez, a 63-year-old Mexican national, was found unresponsive at 9:13 p.m. on June 19, 2026, at the Webb County Detention Center in Laredo, Texas. He was transported by ambulance to Laredo Medical Center, where he was pronounced dead at 10:02 p.m. His official cause of death remains pending an autopsy.

  • Alcorta's death marks at least the 20th ICE custody death in 2026. At this pace, 2026 is on track to surpass 2025's record of 33 deaths — itself the highest total in more than two decades.
  • The Webb County Detention Center, operated by CoreCivic, had been found to have at least nine violations in an ICE inspection in February 2026, approximately one-third of which related to improper medical care. Allegations included staff failing to properly check on detainees for suicide risk and inadequate care for pregnant women.
  • Alcorta is the fifth person to die in Texas ICE detention in 2026. Texas facilities have accounted for at least a quarter of all ICE custody deaths since Trump returned to office.
  • Detention facilities across the country are experiencing increasing overcrowding and understaffing as the Trump administration accelerates interior enforcement, experts note.

The death came just days after HRW and PHR released a joint report finding ICE's mortality rate had more than doubled since Trump's second term began, and just weeks after ICE eliminated its post-release death reporting requirement.
